mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that immediately depress equity valuations over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises gradually instead of sharply.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer confidence and durable goods demand, which compounds corporate revenue growth expectations and drives positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or forward 60-day returns turn negative when both series expand concurrently in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production below 1% month-over-month growth indicate supply-side bottlenecks and falling factory utilization, which compresses corporate profit margins and forces negative equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when industrial production contracts, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.015 over any 2-year window, or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day equity returns during expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading exceeding 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in more than 50% of instances when CPI exceeds the threshold, or the negative correlation between inflation spikes and 60-day returns disappears in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures reveals underlying demand fragility; when consumption lags GDP by more than 0.5%, corporate revenue forecasts are downgraded, causing negative equity returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The consumption-GDP divergence metric shows Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns remain positive despite widening divergence in more than 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is above 2.0% and unemployment remains below 4.0%, the economy operates in a soft-landing equilibrium where stable growth and controlled inflation support steady equity appreciation over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns turn negative in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the joint regime signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.
